metadata.contractTitle: Statement of Work for Software Services
metadata.supplier: CitiusTech Healthcare Technology Private Limited and CitiusTech Inc.
metadata.renewalTerms: Automatic extension by successive three-month periods unless Client notifies to the contrary at least two months prior to the then-current end date
metadata.pricingFull: Time and Materials based on approved effort records at applicable role rates ranging from USD 38/hour to USD 155/hour. Weekend/holiday work at 125% rate. Temporary personnel under three months at 115% rate. Monthly effort variances up to 10% above forecast permitted with operational approval.
metadata.contractValue: Indicative monthly baseline USD 197,005; estimated annual value USD 2,364,060 based on 12-month term
metadata.governingAgreement: Services Agreement dated 15 June 2026 between CitiusTech Inc. and Client
metadata.emLevel: EM1 – Staff Aug: Contract explicitly states Client directs day-to-day work, priorities, schedules, and business acceptance; CitiusTech provides qualified personnel with limited responsibility for final business outputs; no committed output/outcome/milestone/SLA penalties; quality ownership remains with Client for task expectations, detailed acceptance, and production approval; T&M commercial model with flexible operational adjustments.
metadata.billingCadence: Monthly based on approved effort records
metadata.terminationTerms: Governed by this SOW and the Agreement; extension, termination, reduction, or commercial modification governed by SOW and Agreement
metadata.securityTerms: Personnel follow Client security instructions provided in writing; credentials not shared; production access through Client-approved processes; Client data used only for approved business purposes and handled per Agreement
metadata.slaTerms: Profile submission 5 business days for standard roles (remediation discussion only); Replacement initiation 10 business days after approved need (replacement plan); Timesheet accuracy 98% complete by third business day (corrected invoice support)
metadata.scopeChangeTerms: Flexible template permits operational changes through email or workflow tool approval provided no alteration to legal terms, rates, premiums, or minimum notice obligations; material changes require authorized representative approval; emergency operational changes may proceed with written operational approval but must be reconciled commercially
metadata.chargeableEvents: Standard weekday hours at applicable rate; weekend or public holiday work approved in writing at 125% rate; temporary additional personnel under three months at 115% rate; monthly effort variances up to 10% above forecast with operational approval; additional transition support after end date is chargeable unless expressly included
metadata.exitObligations: Orderly transition support including handover meetings, open item inventory, repository updates, runbook updates, knowledge transfer sessions, access return support, and final commercial reconciliation; additional transition after end date is chargeable unless expressly included
metadata.executionEvidence: Execution section present on page 10 with signature blocks for Client, CitiusTech Healthcare Technology Private Limited, and CitiusTech Inc.; actual signatures, names, titles, and dates not filled in uploaded contract

rows.clause: Client directs day-to-day work, priorities, schedules, and business acceptance. CitiusTech is responsible for providing suitable personnel, role continuity support, and reasonable resource management. This is a comparatively flexible template intended for staff capacity review, with limited CitiusTech responsibility for final business outputs.
rows.customerDependency: Client owns task quality expectations, detailed acceptance decisions, production approval, and schedule control
rows.exclusions: CitiusTech has limited responsibility for final business outputs
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm operational governance to ensure clear accountability for delivery outcomes despite staff augmentation model; establish escalation path for quality or delivery disputes
rows.evidence: Client directs day-to-day work, priorities, schedules, and business acceptance. CitiusTech is responsible for providing suitable personnel, role continuity support, and reasonable resource management.
rows.reason: Staff augmentation model places primary delivery control and acceptance responsibility on Client, creating potential ambiguity in accountability for project success or failure

rows.section: Section 3 - Estimated Start and End Dates
rows.clause: The estimated start date is 01 July 2026. The estimated end date is 30 June 2027.
rows.customerDependency: Not stated in uploaded contract
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm start date readiness including access, onboarding, and systems availability to avoid ramp-up delays
rows.evidence: The estimated start date is 01 July 2026. The estimated end date is 30 June 2027.
rows.reason: Clear term definition supports planning; dates are described as estimated which may create ambiguity on binding commitment

rows.section: Section 4 - Team Size, Roles, and Working Calendar
rows.deliverable: Baseline team of 24.5 FTE across 10 roles
rows.clause: Client may adjust the active role mix with 30 days advance written notice. CitiusTech will use commercially reasonable efforts to provide replacement personnel with comparable skills when a role becomes unavailable.
rows.customerDependency: Client must provide 30 days advance written notice for role mix changes
rows.remedy: Commercially reasonable efforts to provide replacement personnel
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify commercial impact of Client-initiated role mix changes within the 30-day notice period; define success criteria for comparable skills in replacement personnel
rows.evidence: Client may adjust the active role mix with 30 days advance written notice. CitiusTech will use commercially reasonable efforts to provide replacement personnel with comparable skills when a role becomes unavailable.
rows.reason: Commercially reasonable efforts standard is subjective and may not guarantee suitable replacement quality or timing; role mix changes could affect delivery continuity

rows.target: Two months prior to then-current end date
rows.clause: The end date of this SOW shall automatically stand extended by successive periods of three months unless otherwise notified by Client to the contrary at least two months prior to the then-current end date.
rows.valueAtRisk: Ongoing monthly baseline USD 197,005 per extension period
rows.customerDependency: Client must notify two months prior to end date to prevent automatic renewal
rows.recommendedAction: Establish calendar reminder system for renewal decision points; clarify whether CitiusTech has reciprocal termination rights or if only Client can prevent renewal
rows.evidence: The end date of this SOW shall automatically stand extended by successive periods of three months unless otherwise notified by Client to the contrary at least two months prior to the then-current end date.
rows.reason: Automatic renewal favors continuity but creates passive commitment risk if Client does not actively manage renewal decisions; asymmetric termination rights unclear

rows.section: Section 6 - Commercial Terms, Billing, and Payment
rows.deliverable: Monthly invoicing with premiums and variance tolerance
rows.target: Up to 10% above forecast with operational approval
rows.measurement: Monthly invoice with supporting effort records
rows.clause: As a flexible review-friendly position, monthly effort variances up to 10% above the forecast may proceed with Client operational approval in email or the project tool, provided that the monthly invoice includes supporting effort records.
rows.valueAtRisk: Up to USD 19,701 per month (10% of baseline)
rows.customerDependency: Client operational approval in email or project tool required for variances
rows.exclusions: Variances beyond 10% require separate approval process
rows.recommendedAction: Define authorized operational approvers and approval authority limits; establish monthly variance tracking and reconciliation process; clarify treatment of cumulative variances over multiple months
rows.evidence: monthly effort variances up to 10% above the forecast may proceed with Client operational approval in email or the project tool, provided that the monthly invoice includes supporting effort records.
rows.reason: 10% monthly variance tolerance with operational approval creates budget predictability risk and potential for scope creep without formal change control; email approval may lack audit trail

rows.section: Section 6 - Commercial Terms, Billing, and Payment
rows.clause: Invoices are payable net 30 days from invoice date. Overdue undisputed amounts may be subject to remedies available under the Agreement.
rows.customerDependency: Client payment within 30 days
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm remedies for overdue payments in governing Agreement including interest rates and suspension rights
rows.evidence: Invoices are payable net 30 days from invoice date. Overdue undisputed amounts may be subject to remedies available under the Agreement.
rows.reason: Standard payment terms; remedies reference to Agreement requires validation of governing document provisions

rows.clause: Clinical decision making, medical judgment, final clinical content approval, and production business sign-off remain Client responsibilities.
rows.customerDependency: Client retains clinical decision making, medical judgment, final clinical content approval, and production business sign-off
rows.exclusions: Hardware procurement, third-party license purchase, independent security certification, formal regulatory submission ownership
rows.recommendedAction: Establish clear handoff protocols between CitiusTech technical deliverables and Client clinical/business approval; document approval evidence requirements to support exclusion claims
rows.evidence: Clinical decision making, medical judgment, final clinical content approval, and production business sign-off remain Client responsibilities.
rows.reason: Healthcare context creates significant liability risk if clinical/business accountability boundaries are not operationally enforced with documented approvals

rows.clause: Any work requiring new platforms, new geography, new legal entity onboarding, or new compliance obligations requires impact assessment and approval.
rows.customerDependency: Client approval for scope expansion
rows.exclusions: New platforms, new geography, new legal entity onboarding, new compliance obligations without impact assessment and approval
rows.recommendedAction: Define impact assessment process, approval authority, and timeline for scope expansion requests
rows.evidence: Any work requiring new platforms, new geography, new legal entity onboarding, or new compliance obligations requires impact assessment and approval.
rows.reason: Protects both parties from unplanned scope expansion; process clarity needed for efficient execution

rows.deliverable: Work outputs subject to Client review
rows.clause: Client owns task quality expectations, detailed acceptance decisions, production approval, and schedule control.
rows.customerDependency: Client must provide timely acceptance decisions with specific reasons for rejection
rows.exclusions: CitiusTech limited responsibility for final business outputs
rows.recommendedAction: Define acceptance criteria upfront for each work stream; establish deemed acceptance period if Client does not respond within defined timeframe; clarify payment entitlement for work rejected without specific defect evidence
rows.evidence: Client owns task quality expectations, detailed acceptance decisions, production approval, and schedule control.
rows.reason: Client acceptance control without defined criteria or deemed acceptance timeline creates payment risk if Client delays or rejects work without clear defect basis

rows.section: Section 11 - Service Levels, Credits, and Remedies
rows.measurement: Business days from request to profile submission
rows.clause: Profile submission 5 business days for standard roles
rows.customerDependency: Approved request and required input available
rows.recommendedAction: Define standard vs. niche skill categories; establish escalation process for profile submission delays
rows.evidence: Profile submission 5 business days for standard roles
rows.reason: SLA has no financial penalty, only remediation discussion; exclusions for niche skills may be subjectively interpreted

rows.section: Section 11 - Service Levels, Credits, and Remedies
rows.measurement: Business days after approved replacement need to replacement plan
rows.clause: Replacement initiation 10 business days after approved replacement need
rows.customerDependency: Approved replacement need
rows.recommendedAction: Define replacement plan content requirements and approval process; clarify treatment of extended notice periods or market scarcity scenarios
rows.evidence: Replacement initiation 10 business days after approved replacement need
rows.reason: SLA has no financial penalty; market scarcity exclusion may be subjectively interpreted and delay critical replacements

rows.section: Section 11 - Service Levels, Credits, and Remedies
rows.measurement: Percentage complete by third business day
rows.clause: Timesheet accuracy 98% complete by third business day
rows.customerDependency: Client tool availability
rows.recommendedAction: Establish timesheet validation and correction process; define invoice correction timeline and approval process
rows.evidence: Timesheet accuracy 98% complete by third business day
rows.reason: Administrative SLA with corrective remedy only; no financial penalty for non-compliance

rows.deliverable: Systems access, environments, test data, approvals
rows.clause: Client will provide timely systems access, VPN, repositories, environments, test data, business contacts, approvals, and workflow tool access.
rows.customerDependency: Client must provide timely systems access, VPN, repositories, environments, test data, business contacts, approvals, and workflow tool access
rows.exclusions: Delays caused by Client dependencies, third-party vendors, environment downtime, or changed priorities will extend affected commitments and exclude impacted measures
rows.remedy: Extension of affected commitments and exclusion of impacted measures
rows.recommendedAction: Establish dependency tracking log with target dates and escalation triggers; define process for documenting Client-caused delays and their commercial impact; clarify whether CitiusTech is entitled to standby charges during Client-caused delays
rows.evidence: Client will provide timely systems access, VPN, repositories, environments, test data, business contacts, approvals, and workflow tool access.
rows.reason: Broad Client dependencies without defined timeliness standards or standby cost recovery mechanism create schedule and cost risk; remedy of commitment extension may not compensate for CitiusTech resource idle time

rows.clause: Client will maintain third-party licenses, vendor support, cloud subscriptions, data availability, and production release authority.
rows.customerDependency: Client must maintain third-party licenses, vendor support, cloud subscriptions, data availability, and production release authority
rows.exclusions: Delays caused by third-party vendors exclude impacted measures
rows.remedy: Extension of affected commitments and exclusion of impacted measures
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain list of required third-party licenses and vendor support agreements; establish validation process to confirm availability before work commencement; define escalation process for license or vendor issues
rows.evidence: Client will maintain third-party licenses, vendor support, cloud subscriptions, data availability, and production release authority.
rows.reason: Third-party dependency failures can block delivery; exclusion remedy does not address CitiusTech cost recovery for delays outside both parties' control

rows.target: Follow Client security instructions provided in writing
rows.clause: CitiusTech personnel will follow Client security instructions provided in writing and applicable to the services. Credentials will not be shared. Production access, where required, will be granted through Client-approved processes. Client data will be used only for approved business purposes and handled in accordance with the Agreement.
rows.valueAtRisk: Security breach liability and regulatory penalties
rows.customerDependency: Client must provide written security instructions
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain comprehensive written security instructions before work commencement; establish security incident reporting and response protocol; validate Agreement provisions for security breach liability, indemnification, and insurance requirements; confirm data handling obligations comply with HIPAA and other healthcare regulations
rows.evidence: CitiusTech personnel will follow Client security instructions provided in writing and applicable to the services. Credentials will not be shared. Production access, where required, will be granted through Client-approved processes. Client data will be used only for approved business purposes and handled in accordance with the Agreement.
rows.reason: Healthcare data security and privacy create significant regulatory and liability exposure; reliance on written instructions and Agreement terms requires validation of adequacy and enforceability; breach could result in substantial penalties and reputational damage

rows.target: Email or workflow tool approval for operational changes
rows.clause: This flexible template permits operational changes through email or workflow tool approval, provided such changes do not alter legal terms, rates, premiums, or minimum notice obligations.
rows.valueAtRisk: Scope creep and unauthorized cost increases
rows.customerDependency: Client operational approval required
rows.exclusions: Changes altering legal terms, rates, premiums, or minimum notice obligations require authorized representative approval
rows.recommendedAction: Define authorized operational approvers and their approval limits; establish change request template requiring business reason, scope impact, commercial impact, milestone impact, and acceptance impact; implement change log tracking and monthly reconciliation; clarify boundary between operational changes and material changes requiring authorized representative approval
rows.evidence: This flexible template permits operational changes through email or workflow tool approval, provided such changes do not alter legal terms, rates, premiums, or minimum notice obligations.
rows.reason: Informal email approval process creates audit trail and accountability gaps; boundary between operational and material changes may be subjectively interpreted leading to unauthorized scope expansion

rows.target: Written operational approval with commercial reconciliation
rows.clause: Emergency operational changes may proceed with written operational approval but must be reconciled commercially if they affect charges or commitments.
rows.customerDependency: Client operational approval and commercial reconciliation
rows.recommendedAction: Define emergency change criteria and approval authority; establish maximum time period for commercial reconciliation; clarify treatment of emergency changes where commercial reconciliation is disputed or delayed
rows.evidence: Emergency operational changes may proceed with written operational approval but must be reconciled commercially if they affect charges or commitments.
rows.reason: Emergency changes without upfront commercial agreement create payment dispute risk; reconciliation process and timeline not defined

rows.target: CitiusTech retains ownership; Client receives royalty-free internal use license
rows.clause: CitiusTech retains ownership of such pre-existing assets and grants Client a royalty-free right to use embedded assets solely as part of the project outputs for Client internal business purposes.
rows.customerDependency: Client limited to internal business use
rows.exclusions: External use, resale, or licensing to third parties
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify definition of embedded assets vs. standalone accelerators; confirm Client can modify embedded assets for internal use; establish process for identifying and documenting pre-existing IP in deliverables
rows.evidence: CitiusTech retains ownership of such pre-existing assets and grants Client a royalty-free right to use embedded assets solely as part of the project outputs for Client internal business purposes.
rows.reason: Standard IP protection for vendor accelerators; internal use limitation is reasonable but requires clear boundary definition

rows.deliverable: Payment for services through termination date
rows.target: Payment for all services performed through effective date of expiry or termination
rows.clause: CHTPL and CitiusTech Inc. each shall be entitled to payment for all respective services performed, accepted milestones, approved output units, approved outcome-linked amounts, reimbursable expenses, and transition support through the effective date of expiry or termination.
rows.customerDependency: Client acceptance and approval of final invoices
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify treatment of work in progress at termination; define acceptance process and timeline for final deliverables; confirm transition support scope and duration included vs. chargeable; establish final invoice submission and payment timeline
rows.evidence: CHTPL and CitiusTech Inc. each shall be entitled to payment for all respective services performed, accepted milestones, approved output units, approved outcome-linked amounts, reimbursable expenses, and transition support through the effective date of expiry or termination.
rows.reason: Payment entitlement references accepted milestones and output units which are not defined in this T&M staff augmentation SOW, creating potential dispute over final payment; transition support scope and chargeability unclear

rows.section: Annexure E - Completion, Transition, and Exit
rows.deliverable: Orderly transition including handovers, documentation, knowledge transfer
rows.clause: Upon expiry, termination, reduction, or completion, CitiusTech will provide orderly transition support. Transition may include handover meetings, open item inventory, repository updates, runbook updates, knowledge transfer sessions, access return support, and final commercial reconciliation. Additional transition after the end date is chargeable unless expressly included.
rows.customerDependency: Client participation in transition activities
rows.exclusions: Additional transition after end date is chargeable unless expressly included
rows.recommendedAction: Define transition period duration and activities included in base contract vs. chargeable; establish transition plan template and approval process; clarify knowledge transfer requirements and evidence; confirm access return and data deletion obligations
rows.evidence: Upon expiry, termination, reduction, or completion, CitiusTech will provide orderly transition support. Transition may include handover meetings, open item inventory, repository updates, runbook updates, knowledge transfer sessions, access return support, and final commercial reconciliation. Additional transition after the end date is chargeable unless expressly included.
rows.reason: Transition scope is illustrative (may include) rather than committed; boundary between included and chargeable transition support is unclear; inadequate transition could result in knowledge loss and operational disruption

rows.clause: This SOW is executed by duly authorized representatives of the parties as of the last date of signature below.
rows.customerDependency: Client authorized representative signature
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm signatory authority for all parties before work commencement; obtain fully executed copy with all signature blocks completed including names, titles, and dates; validate that signatories have authority to bind their respective entities
rows.evidence: This SOW is executed by duly authorized representatives of the parties as of the last date of signature below.
rows.reason: Uploaded contract shows signature blocks but no actual signatures, names, titles, or dates; contract may not be legally binding without proper execution; work should not commence without validated execution

rows.deliverable: Services from CHTPL (offshore) and CitiusTech Inc. (onsite)
rows.target: Clear entity accountability for offshore vs. onsite services
rows.clause: CitiusTech Healthcare Technology Private Limited shall be responsible for performance and invoicing for offshore services. CitiusTech Inc. shall be responsible for performance and invoicing for onsite services. Pending completion of onboarding of the relevant service provider entity in Client systems, any other duly onboarded CitiusTech entity may issue invoices and collect payments on behalf of such service provider entity on an interim basis.
rows.customerDependency: Client must onboard both entities in payment systems
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm both entities are properly onboarded in Client vendor and payment systems before work commencement; clarify liability allocation between entities for service failures; establish clear invoicing protocol to avoid payment confusion; validate tax and withholding treatment for each entity
rows.evidence: CitiusTech Healthcare Technology Private Limited shall be responsible for performance and invoicing for offshore services. CitiusTech Inc. shall be responsible for performance and invoicing for onsite services. Pending completion of onboarding of the relevant service provider entity in Client systems, any other duly onboarded CitiusTech entity may issue invoices and collect payments on behalf of such service provider entity on an interim basis.
rows.reason: Multi-entity structure creates complexity in payment routing, liability allocation, and tax treatment; interim invoicing arrangement may cause payment and reconciliation issues

rows.section: Section 6 - Commercial Terms, Billing, and Payment
rows.service: Weekend, Holiday, and Temporary Personnel Premiums
rows.target: 125% for weekend/holiday work; 115% for temporary personnel under 3 months
rows.measurement: Approved effort records with premium justification
rows.clause: Weekend or public holiday work approved in writing is billed at 125% of the applicable rate. Temporary additional personnel assigned for less than three months are billed at 115% of the applicable rate unless otherwise agreed in writing.
rows.customerDependency: Client written approval required for weekend/holiday work
rows.recommendedAction: Establish approval process and authority for weekend/holiday work; define public holiday calendar (US, India, or both); clarify whether temporary personnel premium applies to Client-requested short-term additions or CitiusTech-initiated replacements; implement tracking for premium charges
rows.evidence: Weekend or public holiday work approved in writing is billed at 125% of the applicable rate. Temporary additional personnel assigned for less than three months are billed at 115% of the applicable rate unless otherwise agreed in writing.
rows.reason: Premium rates are standard but require clear approval process to control costs; temporary personnel premium definition may be ambiguous

boardSummary.statement: EM1 engagement with Client controlling work direction, acceptance, and schedule; limited vendor output accountability
Healthcare data handling with security instructions dependency; Agreement validation required for breach liability and HIPAA compliance
Contract signature blocks present but not executed; work should not commence without validated authorized signatures
10% monthly variance tolerance and email-based change approval create budget predictability and scope creep risk
Broad Client obligations for access, environments, licenses, and approvals without timeliness standards or standby cost recovery

proceedIf.statement: Obtain fully executed contract with authorized signatures, names, titles, and dates from all parties before work commencement
Confirm Agreement provisions for security breach liability, indemnification, insurance, and HIPAA compliance are adequate and enforceable
Verify both CitiusTech entities are onboarded in Client vendor and payment systems with clear invoicing and tax treatment
Validate Client readiness to provide timely access, environments, test data, licenses, and approvals per dependency tracking log
Define authorized operational approvers, change control process, acceptance criteria, and monthly variance reconciliation before start date

renegotiate.statement: Add financial service credits or penalties for profile submission, replacement initiation, and timesheet accuracy SLAs to strengthen accountability
Establish deemed acceptance period if Client does not respond to deliverables within defined timeframe to reduce payment dispute risk
Negotiate standby or delay charges when Client dependencies cause resource idle time to protect against uncompensated delays
Define included transition period duration and activities vs. chargeable transition support to avoid exit cost disputes
Reduce monthly effort variance tolerance from 10% to 5% and require formal change control for cumulative variances to improve budget predictability

financialNotes.statement: Indicative monthly baseline USD 197,005 based on 24.5 FTE across 10 roles at stated rates and hours
Estimated annual value USD 2,364,060 calculated as monthly baseline × 12 months per stated term 01-Jul-2026 to 30-Jun-2027
Full annual value at risk due to execution incompleteness, security exposure, Client dependency failures, and flexible variance controls
No service credits or financial penalties stated for SLA failures; remedies limited to remediation discussion, replacement plan, and corrected invoice support
Weekend/holiday work at 125% rate and temporary personnel under 3 months at 115% rate create variable cost exposure requiring approval controls
Net 30 day payment terms with remedies per Agreement; multi-entity invoicing structure and interim payment arrangements require validation

revenueControls.title: Monthly Effort Variance Tracking
revenueControls.detail: Implement tracking and reconciliation process for monthly effort variances up to 10% above forecast with operational approval; establish cumulative variance monitoring and escalation triggers
revenueControls.owner: Client Finance and CitiusTech Delivery Manager

revenueControls.title: Premium Rate Approval Process
revenueControls.detail: Establish written approval process and authority limits for weekend/holiday work at 125% rate and temporary personnel at 115% rate; define public holiday calendar and premium applicability rules

revenueControls.title: Change Control Log
revenueControls.detail: Maintain change log tracking all operational changes approved via email or workflow tool; implement monthly reconciliation to validate no unauthorized scope expansion or rate changes
revenueControls.owner: Client Program Manager and CitiusTech Program Manager

revenueControls.title: Dependency Tracking and Escalation
revenueControls.detail: Establish dependency tracking log with target dates for Client-provided access, environments, licenses, and approvals; define escalation triggers and process for documenting Client-caused delays

revenueControls.title: Invoice Validation Process
revenueControls.detail: Implement invoice validation process to verify effort records, rate application, premium justification, and variance approvals before payment; establish dispute resolution timeline

commercialBaseline.interpretation: Monthly invoicing based on approved effort records at role-specific hourly rates; no fixed-price, output, or outcome commitments
Indicative monthly value based on 24.5 FTE across 10 roles at stated rates and hours per Annexure B
Estimated annual value calculated as monthly baseline × 12 months per stated 12-month term
Role-specific rates from Software Engineer (USD 38/hour) to Solution Architect (USD 155/hour) with offshore and onsite differentiation
Monthly effort variances up to 10% above forecast permitted with Client operational approval; creates budget predictability risk
Invoices payable net 30 days from invoice date; overdue undisputed amounts subject to Agreement remedies
No financial service credits or penalties for SLA failures; remedies limited to remediation discussion and corrective actions
Automatic renewal by successive 3-month periods unless Client notifies to contrary at least 2 months prior to end date

leakageScenarios.scenario: Unauthorized Scope Expansion via Email Approval
leakageScenarios.impact: 10% monthly variance tolerance (USD 19,701/month) plus informal email-based change approval enables scope creep without formal change control
leakageScenarios.control: Implement change log tracking all email/tool approvals; monthly reconciliation to validate no unauthorized scope or rate changes; define authorized operational approvers and limits
leakageScenarios.evidence: Change control log, monthly reconciliation report, approval authority matrix

leakageScenarios.scenario: Client Dependency Delays Without Cost Recovery
leakageScenarios.impact: Client delays in providing access, environments, licenses, or approvals cause CitiusTech resource idle time without standby charge recovery mechanism
leakageScenarios.control: Establish dependency tracking log with target dates and escalation triggers; document Client-caused delays and their duration; negotiate standby or delay charges for future amendments
leakageScenarios.evidence: Dependency tracking log, delay documentation, escalation records

leakageScenarios.impact: Client owns acceptance decisions without defined criteria or deemed acceptance timeline; rejected work without specific defect evidence creates payment dispute risk
leakageScenarios.control: Define acceptance criteria upfront for each work stream; establish deemed acceptance period (e.g., 5 business days); require specific defect evidence for rejections
leakageScenarios.evidence: Acceptance criteria documentation, approval notes, rejection reasons with defect evidence
leakageScenarios.owner: Client Manager and CitiusTech Delivery Lead

leakageScenarios.impact: Weekend/holiday work at 125% rate and temporary personnel at 115% rate create variable cost exposure if approval controls are weak
leakageScenarios.control: Establish written approval process and authority limits for premium work; define public holiday calendar; track and report premium charges monthly
leakageScenarios.evidence: Premium work approval records, public holiday calendar, monthly premium charge report

leakageScenarios.impact: Transition scope is illustrative rather than committed; boundary between included and chargeable transition support unclear; inadequate transition planning could result in unexpected exit costs
leakageScenarios.control: Define included transition period duration and activities vs. chargeable support; establish transition plan template and approval process; budget for transition costs
leakageScenarios.evidence: Transition plan, transition activity log, transition cost tracking
leakageScenarios.owner: Client Sponsor and CitiusTech Account Lead

penaltyExposure.High: No financial service credits or penalties stated for High-risk items including security breach, execution incompleteness, or Client dependency failures. Remedies governed by referenced Agreement which requires validation.
penaltyExposure.Medium: No financial penalties for Medium-risk items including SLA failures (profile submission, replacement initiation, timesheet accuracy), acceptance delays, change control gaps, or transition scope ambiguity. Remedies limited to remediation discussion, replacement plan, corrected invoice support, and commitment extensions.
penaltyExposure.Low: No financial penalties for Low-risk items. Standard administrative and governance controls apply.

recos.action: Strengthen security and data handling obligations with explicit HIPAA compliance, breach notification, liability caps, and insurance requirements
recos.original: CitiusTech personnel will follow Client security instructions provided in writing and applicable to the services. Client data will be used only for approved business purposes and handled in accordance with the Agreement.
recos.rewrite: CitiusTech personnel will follow Client security instructions provided in writing and comply with HIPAA, HITECH, and applicable healthcare data protection regulations. CitiusTech will implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards to protect Client data and PHI. Security breaches must be reported to Client within 24 hours of discovery. CitiusTech will maintain cyber liability insurance of at least USD 5,000,000 per occurrence. Liability for security breaches caused by CitiusTech negligence is capped at 12 months of fees paid under this SOW, except for breaches involving willful misconduct or gross negligence. Client data will be used only for approved business purposes, encrypted in transit and at rest, and deleted or returned within 30 days of contract termination.

recos.action: Add deemed acceptance provision to reduce payment dispute risk when Client delays acceptance decisions
recos.original: Client owns task quality expectations, detailed acceptance decisions, production approval, and schedule control.
recos.rewrite: Client owns task quality expectations, detailed acceptance decisions, production approval, and schedule control. Client will review deliverables and provide acceptance decision or specific rejection reasons within 5 business days of submission. If Client does not respond within 5 business days, the deliverable will be deemed accepted. Rejections must include specific defect evidence tied to agreed acceptance criteria. CitiusTech is entitled to payment for deemed accepted deliverables.

recos.action: Add standby charge provision to compensate CitiusTech for resource idle time caused by Client dependency delays
recos.original: Delays caused by Client dependencies, third-party vendors, environment downtime, or changed priorities will extend affected commitments and exclude impacted measures.
recos.rewrite: Delays caused by Client dependencies, third-party vendors, environment downtime, or changed priorities will extend affected commitments and exclude impacted measures. If Client dependency delays exceed 3 business days and cause CitiusTech resource idle time, Client will pay standby charges at 50% of the applicable hourly rate for affected resources during the delay period. CitiusTech will document delays in the dependency tracking log and provide weekly delay reports to Client.

recos.contract: Section 11 - Service Levels, Credits, and Remedies
recos.action: Add financial service credits to strengthen accountability for SLA failures
recos.original: Profile submission 5 business days for standard roles - Remediation discussion only. Replacement initiation 10 business days after approved replacement need - Replacement plan. Timesheet accuracy 98% complete by third business day - Corrected invoice support.
recos.rewrite: Profile submission 5 business days for standard roles - Service credit of USD 500 per business day of delay beyond target, capped at USD 2,500 per occurrence. Replacement initiation 10 business days after approved replacement need - Service credit of USD 1,000 per business day of delay beyond target, capped at USD 5,000 per occurrence. Timesheet accuracy 98% complete by third business day - Service credit of USD 250 per percentage point below 98%, capped at USD 1,000 per month. Exclusions: Niche skills, Client delay, notice period, market scarcity, Client tool outage.

recos.contract: Section 6 - Commercial Terms, Billing, and Payment
recos.action: Reduce monthly effort variance tolerance and require formal change control for cumulative variances to improve budget predictability
recos.original: As a flexible review-friendly position, monthly effort variances up to 10% above the forecast may proceed with Client operational approval in email or the project tool, provided that the monthly invoice includes supporting effort records.
recos.rewrite: Monthly effort variances up to 5% above the forecast may proceed with Client operational approval in email or the project tool, provided that the monthly invoice includes supporting effort records. Variances between 5% and 10% require written approval from Client authorized representative. Variances exceeding 10% or cumulative variances exceeding 10% over any 3-month period require formal change control approval per Section 14. CitiusTech will provide monthly variance reports showing actual vs. forecast consumption and cumulative variance trends.

recos.contract: Annexure E - Completion, Transition, and Exit
recos.action: Define included transition period and activities to avoid exit cost disputes
recos.original: Upon expiry, termination, reduction, or completion, CitiusTech will provide orderly transition support. Transition may include handover meetings, open item inventory, repository updates, runbook updates, knowledge transfer sessions, access return support, and final commercial reconciliation. Additional transition after the end date is chargeable unless expressly included.
recos.rewrite: Upon expiry, termination, reduction, or completion, CitiusTech will provide orderly transition support for a period of 30 days following the end date at no additional charge. Included transition activities are: up to 10 handover meetings, open item inventory, repository updates, runbook updates, up to 20 hours of knowledge transfer sessions per major work stream, access return support, and final commercial reconciliation. Transition activities beyond the included scope or duration will be charged at the applicable hourly rates. Client must provide a transition plan and participate actively in transition activities. CitiusTech will provide a transition completion report documenting all activities performed.

recos.action: Strengthen change control process to prevent unauthorized scope expansion and improve audit trail
recos.original: This flexible template permits operational changes through email or workflow tool approval, provided such changes do not alter legal terms, rates, premiums, or minimum notice obligations. Emergency operational changes may proceed with written operational approval but must be reconciled commercially if they affect charges or commitments.
recos.rewrite: Operational changes not altering legal terms, rates, premiums, or minimum notice obligations may be approved by Client authorized operational approvers via email or workflow tool. All change requests must describe business reason, scope impact, commercial impact, milestone impact, acceptance impact, and approval authority. CitiusTech will maintain a change control log tracking all approved changes and provide monthly change summaries. Emergency operational changes may proceed with written operational approval but must be reconciled commercially within 10 business days if they affect charges or commitments. Material changes altering legal terms, rates, premiums, minimum notice obligations, or cumulative commercial impact exceeding USD 25,000 require written approval from authorized representatives of both parties.

assumptions.assumption: Analysis based solely on uploaded 10-page SOW document. Governing Services Agreement dated 15 June 2026 referenced throughout but not uploaded; critical terms including security breach liability, indemnification, insurance, termination rights, dispute resolution, and IP ownership require validation in the Agreement. Contract signature blocks present but not executed; actual signatures, names, titles, and dates not available in uploaded document.

Governing Services Agreement dated 15 June 2026 between CitiusTech Inc. and Client is referenced as controlling document but not uploaded. Multiple SOW provisions state terms are governed by the Agreement including security obligations, payment remedies, termination terms, taxes, withholding, reimbursable expenses, and subcontractor procedures. Risk assessment and recommendations note Agreement validation required; confidence scores reduced where SOW relies on Agreement terms.

Contract value calculated as indicative monthly baseline USD 197,005 (per Annexure B) × 12 months = USD 2,364,060 annual value based on stated 12-month term 01-Jul-2026 to 30-Jun-2027. Monthly baseline derived from 24.5 FTE across 10 roles at stated hourly rates and average monthly hours. Actual value will vary based on approved effort records, role mix changes, premium rates, and variance approvals. Automatic 3-month renewal provisions could extend value beyond initial 12-month term.

Contract value at risk allocated by risk level based on row counts: High 50% (USD 1,182,030 for 1 High row), Medium 40% (USD 945,624 for 15 Medium rows), Low 10% (USD 236,406 for 7 Low rows). Allocation reflects that High-risk security exposure could jeopardize entire contract value; Medium risks create operational and commercial exposure requiring management controls; Low risks are administrative with limited financial impact. No service credits or financial penalties stated, so value at risk represents potential contract loss or dispute exposure rather than liquidated damages.

EM1 – Staff Aug classification based on explicit contract language: Client directs day-to-day work, priorities, schedules, and business acceptance; CitiusTech provides qualified personnel with limited responsibility for final business outputs; Client owns task quality expectations, detailed acceptance decisions, production approval, and schedule control; Time & Materials commercial model with monthly invoicing based on approved effort records; no committed output, outcome, milestone, or SLA penalties. SLAs present (profile submission, replacement initiation, timesheet accuracy) have no financial penalties, only remediation discussion or corrective actions, consistent with EM1 resource management obligations rather than EM3/EM4 delivery commitments.

Commercial baseline derived from Annexure B Sample Monthly Forecast showing 24.5 FTE across 10 roles with role-specific hourly rates and average monthly hours. Monthly baseline USD 197,005 represents indicative value subject to actual approved effort records, role mix changes per Section 4, premium rates per Section 6, and monthly variance tolerance up to 10% with operational approval. Annual value USD 2,364,060 calculated as monthly baseline × 12 months per stated term; actual annual value will vary based on effort consumption, renewals, and changes.

Revenue protection controls focus on areas where contract flexibility creates leakage risk: monthly effort variance tracking (10% tolerance with operational approval), premium rate approval process (125% weekend/holiday, 115% temporary personnel), change control log (email/tool approvals), dependency tracking (Client-caused delays), and invoice validation (effort records, rate application, variance approvals). Controls designed to prevent unauthorized scope expansion, premium overuse, Client dependency cost absorption, and payment disputes. No service credits or penalties stated, so controls emphasize documentation, approval processes, and escalation rather than financial remedies.

Leakage scenarios derived from contract flexibility provisions and control gaps: unauthorized scope expansion via 10% variance tolerance and email-based change approval; Client dependency delays without standby charge recovery; acceptance delays and payment disputes without deemed acceptance; premium rate overuse without strong approval controls; transition cost overruns due to scope ambiguity. Each scenario quantifies potential impact, identifies required controls, specifies evidence requirements, and assigns ownership. Scenarios reflect staff augmentation model where Client controls work direction but contract lacks strong cost recovery mechanisms for Client-caused delays or scope changes.

Confidence scores reflect evidence quality and completeness in uploaded SOW. High confidence (0.90-0.98) for terms explicitly stated with clear language and supporting annexures. Medium confidence (0.85-0.89) for terms requiring interpretation, referencing unuploaded Agreement, or containing subjective standards (commercially reasonable efforts, timely provision, orderly transition). Lower confidence where critical terms depend on Agreement validation (security breach liability, payment remedies, termination rights) or execution evidence is incomplete (signature blocks present but not executed). Confidence scoring supports risk-based prioritization of validation activities before contract execution.

Page numbers derived from [[PAGE n]] markers in uploaded contract text. Each risk row's pageNo field set to the number from the nearest preceding page marker above the quoted evidence. Page numbers support evidence location and validation. For multi-page sections, pageNo reflects the page where the specific quoted evidence appears. Annexures assigned to their stated page numbers (Annexure A page 5, Annexure B page 6, etc.). Page number accuracy depends on marker placement in source document; manual validation recommended for critical clauses.

Contract text appears to be clean PDF extraction with clear formatting and complete content. No obvious OCR errors, garbled text, or missing sections detected. However, execution section (page 10) shows signature blocks without actual signatures, names, titles, or dates, indicating uploaded document may be a template or unsigned draft. Manual validation required to: confirm contract is fully executed by authorized representatives; validate governing Services Agreement dated 15 June 2026 contains adequate security, liability, indemnification, and termination provisions; verify both CitiusTech entities are onboarded in Client vendor and payment systems; confirm Client readiness to provide dependencies (access, environments, licenses, approvals) per stated start date 01-Jul-2026.

SOW explicitly incorporates Services Agreement dated 15 June 2026 by reference and states multiple critical terms are governed by the Agreement: security obligations and data handling, payment remedies for overdue invoices, termination terms and rights, taxes and withholding treatment, reimbursable expenses, subcontractor procedures, and dispute resolution. Without the Agreement, cannot validate: security breach liability caps and insurance requirements; indemnification scope and limitations; termination notice periods and wind-down obligations; IP ownership for work product vs. pre-existing accelerators; dispute resolution and governing law; limitation of liability and consequential damages exclusions. All High and Medium risks noting Agreement dependency require manual validation of governing document before contract execution and work commencement.
